title: Citrusy Crumbly Uruguayan Peach Crumble
description: This paleo-friendly weeknight dish features juicy peaches pressure-cooked with a citrusy crumbly topping for a delicious dessert or snack.

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup coconut flour or almond flour
3 tbsp melted coconut oil or avocado oil

instructions: Preheat your pressure cooker to high heat.
Wash and halve the peach, removing any pits. Arrange halves in the bottom of the pressure cooker.
Add honey or nectar, lemons (zest only), and lemon juice to the peach halves. Seal the cooker and cook for 15 minutes on high pressure.
While the cooker is under pressure, mix coconut flour with melted oil until smooth and creamy.
Once cooked, let your pressure cooker naturally release steam for about 10 minutes before carefully opening it. Remove peaches and juice from cooker and set aside.
In a mixing bowl, combine the cooled peach mixture with the coconut crumb mixture until well combined.
Pour the mixed crumble over the remaining halves of peaches in the pressure cooker or into a baking dish. Smooth out evenly.
Bake at 350°F (175°C) for about 20 minutes, or until golden and crispy on top. Serve warm as a dessert or snack.
